title: DocMed Landing Page
content:
This file defines the main landing page for the DocMed application.

## Purpose

The `app/page.tsx` file is responsible for rendering the entire landing page of the DocMed website. It orchestrates various components to present the product's value proposition, features, and calls to action to potential users.

## Key Components & Features

-   **`Navbar`**: A fixed navigation bar at the top of the page, featuring the brand logo, navigation links (Features, How it Works, Pricing, Docs), and authentication buttons (Log in, Get Started). It includes a mobile-responsive menu toggle.
-   **`Hero`**: The primary section of the landing page, showcasing a dynamic globe animation, a compelling headline about self-healing documentation, and subtext explaining the core benefit. It also includes prominent call-to-action buttons for starting a free trial and watching a demo.
-   **`TrustBar`**: A section displaying logos of reputable companies that trust DocMed, reinforcing credibility.
-   **`Features`**: Highlights the core functionalities of DocMed, such as the Auto-Sync Engine, AI Doc Generation, Version Healing, and Universal Integrations. Each feature is presented with an icon, title, description, and a key benefit.
-   **`HowItWorks`**: Details the step-by-step process of integrating and using DocMed, from connecting a repository to AI-generated and self-healing documentation.
-   **`Stats`**: Presents key metrics and benefits of using DocMed, such as reduction in stale docs, setup time, and uptime SLA.
-   **`CTA` (Call to Action)**: A prominent section encouraging users to end documentation rot by signing up for a free trial. It includes an email input field and a submit button.
-   **`Footer`**: The bottom section of the page, containing branding, navigation links organized by category (Product, Developers, Company), and legal information.

## Usage Example

This file is the entry point for the root route (`/`) of the application. When a user navigates to the application's base URL, this component is rendered, displaying the complete landing page experience. No direct import or usage within other components is intended for this file, as it serves as the primary page render.

```typescript
// app/page.tsx
// ... imports and component definitions ...

export default function Home() {
  return (
    <main>
      <Navbar />
      <Hero />
      <TrustBar />
      <Features />
      <HowItWorks />
      <Stats />
      <CTA />
      <Footer />
    </main>
  );
}
```
